content.text:
[cron:feee69f8-6016-4f8f-a09c-08b39d9a9191 github-backup] Run these commands: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push). Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.
Current time: Monday, April 13th, 2026 - 2:01 AM (UTC) / 2026-04-13 02:01 UTC

content.thinking:
This is a cron job that runs git backup commands. Let me analyze what's being asked:

1. cd to workspace
2. git add -A (stage all changes)
3. git diff --cached --quiet (check if anything staged)
4. If quiet (nothing staged) → echo "nothing to commit"
5. Otherwise → commit and push

The instruction says: Report to Nemesis ONLY if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.

This is a cron job - I should execute it and only message if there's an actual error (like git push failing). If nothing changed, I should send NO_REPLY (which means no response at all per my rules).

Let me execute this.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing_to_commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push)
